title: Pav Bhaji

overview.description: Mumbai's famous spiced vegetable mash served with buttery toasted bread rolls. A fantastic way to turn everyday vegetables into a rich, comforting meal.

ingredients: 8 pav rolls or soft bread rolls
method: Boil the potatoes, carrots and cauliflower until very tender.
Add the peas for the final 3 minutes.
Mash thoroughly until mostly smooth, leaving a little texture.
Heat the oil and butter over a medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and cook for 30 seconds.
Cook the onions for 8–10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, ginger and green chillies and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the Pav Bhaji Masala and Kashmiri chilli powder and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the tomatoes and cook until thickened, about 10 minutes.
Add a splash of water if needed to achieve a soft, scoopable consistency.
Stir in the lemon juice and fresh coriander.
Serve with extra butter, chopped onion, coriander and lemon wedges.

learning.did-you-know: Pav Bhaji originated in Mumbai as a quick meal for textile mill workers and has become one of India's best-loved street foods.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build flavour from onions and spices before incorporating a mixed vegetable mash.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A brilliant budget meal that turns everyday vegetables into something far more exciting and freezes beautifully.
chapman-tips: Pressure cook the vegetables for 8 minutes to save time before mashing.
The vegetables should be mashed, not blended—you want a little texture.
Pav Bhaji Masala is worth buying rather than blending your own for this recipe.
Finish with plenty of butter and lemon just before serving.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the bhaji in family-sized portions. Toast fresh pav when serving.
notes.leftovers: Excellent as a baked potato topping or served with rice.
